prediction.summary: A service account, svc_backup, was used to perform a series of suspicious activities across multiple internal hosts. The account initially accessed a file share on FILESRV01, reading 842 files, then performed RDP logins to JUMP01 and subsequently to DBSRV02. On DBSRV02, the account executed sqlcmd against the master database, indicating potential data exfiltration or manipulation. This chain of events was detected as lateral movement using remote services (MITRE T1021) by the SIEM and IDS.

timeline.event: Account svc_backup logs into dc-winlog (likely FILESRV01) from 10.10.5.12.
SMB session opened by svc_backup on filesrv to \\FILESRV01\finance$ from 10.10.5.12.
RDP login success by svc_backup to JUMP01 from 10.10.5.12.
RDP login success by svc_backup from JUMP01 to DBSRV02.
Interactive logon by svc_backup on DBSRV02 from JUMP01.
sqlcmd executed by svc_backup against master database on DBSRV02 from JUMP01.
SIEM detects lateral movement chain: FILESRV01 -> JUMP01 -> DBSRV02 via svc_backup.
SIEM observes MITRE technique T1021 Remote Services across 3 internal hosts.
Firewall observes internal east-west traffic spike 10.10.5.12 -> 10.10.8.20 port 445.
Firewall observes internal east-west traffic 10.10.8.20 -> 10.10.12.5 port 3389.
IDS detects suspicious pivot: service account svc_backup authenticating on 3 hosts in 2 minutes.
SIEM alerts HIGH severity for lateral movement by svc_backup.

recommendations: Verify whether the svc_backup account's activity, particularly the RDP logins and sqlcmd execution, was authorized or expected. Confirm with the relevant team (e.g., IT operations, database administrators).
Immediately reset credentials for the svc_backup account and revoke all active sessions to contain potential further compromise.
Investigate all commands and processes executed by svc_backup on FILESRV01, JUMP01, and DBSRV02 to understand the full scope of compromise and identify any additional malicious actions.
Review access controls for the finance$ share on FILESRV01 and the master database on DBSRV02 to ensure the principle of least privilege is enforced for the svc_backup account.
Correlate with Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) alerts on all involved hosts (FILESRV01, JUMP01, DBSRV02) for any additional malicious activity or indicators of compromise.
